name: Campe
description: Campe was a monstrous female creature in Greek mythology, known for her role as the jailer of the Cyclopes and Hecatoncheires in Tartarus. She was a fearsome being, described with a grotesque appearance befitting her monstrous nature and her position as a guardian of the deepest, darkest prison in the underworld. Campe was tasked by Cronus to keep these powerful beings imprisoned, fearing their strength and potential to overthrow him. She was eventually slain by Zeus when he freed the Cyclopes and Hecatoncheires to aid him in the Titanomachy, the epic war between the Titans and the Olympian gods. Campe's demise marked a turning point in the war, as the freed giants played a crucial role in securing victory for Zeus and the Olympians. While details about her specific powers and abilities are scarce, her role as a jailer suggests considerable strength, resilience, and likely some form of magical ability to contain such powerful beings. She embodies the primal fear and chaos that preceded the reign of the Olympian gods.
subtitle: The fearsome jailer of Tartarus
appearance: Campe is described as a monstrous female creature with a terrifying appearance. Details vary, but she is often depicted with serpentine features, such as snakes growing from her head or body. She possesses the torso of a woman but has the lower body of a scorpion or other monstrous creature. She has wings, allowing her to fly and survey her domain. She also possesses numerous animal heads sprouting from her body, representing various beasts and monsters.
habitat: Tartarus, the deepest level of the Underworld, serving as a prison for the Cyclopes and Hecatoncheires.
behavior: Campe is depicted as a vigilant and ruthless jailer, dedicated to her duty of keeping the Cyclopes and Hecatoncheires imprisoned. She is fiercely protective of her charge and would likely attack anyone who attempted to interfere with her duties. Her behavior is driven by loyalty to Cronus and a deep understanding of the threat posed by the imprisoned giants.

keyMyths.title: Campe's Imprisonment
keyMyths.description: Campe was appointed by Cronus to guard the Cyclopes and Hecatoncheires in Tartarus, ensuring they remained imprisoned and unable to challenge his rule. Her role was crucial in maintaining Cronus's power and preventing chaos from erupting in the cosmos.

keyMyths.title: Campe's Defeat
keyMyths.description: Zeus, seeking to overthrow Cronus, descended into Tartarus and slew Campe, freeing the Cyclopes and Hecatoncheires. These giants then aided Zeus in the Titanomachy, providing him with weapons and strength that proved decisive in the war.

sources.author: Pseudo-Apollodorus
sources.description: A comprehensive compendium of Greek myths and legends, providing details about Campe's role and fate.
sources.text: A comprehensive compendium of Greek myths and legends, providing details about Campe's role and fate.
